summary: Domain transformations import rendering.formulas, violating the intended hexagonal boundary.
detail: Rohliste marks D-001 as the symptom and IR-002 as the fix location for the same layer-boundary issue.
fix_comment: Formula intent was moved from rendering to core and the domain/core -> rendering/io_backends boundary is now guard-protected. REVIEW-002 marks D-001 + IR-002 resolved and guarded.

summary: core/fk.py carries FK naming convention and helper primitives that belong to domain ownership.
detail: Clustered with FK naming overlap, backward-compatibility residue, and FK step-name inconsistency.
fix_comment: The original domain/fk move target was superseded by the later FK-helper lifecycle redesign: primitives became policy-driven, FK helper runtime moved to domain-owned packages, and core.fk is retained deliberately as leaf/wire-format logic. This is not a literal domain/fk extraction, but the coupling risk is no longer the same issue.

summary: FK-related modules have overlapping names across core, transformations, validations, and helpers.
detail: The consolidation notes D-003 and D-002 as related and sharing a likely domain/fk package fix.
fix_comment: REVIEW-002 already classified the name-overlap risk as mitigated: core.fk is leaf primitives, domain/transformations/fk_helpers owns lifecycle, and domain/validations/fk_helpers owns checks. The later FK-helper consolidation made that split intentional and documented, so the original D-003 risk is superseded rather than literally renamed away.

summary: Several domain modules exceed the practical read-window size for humans and agents.
detail: Cluster C7 groups module-size debt with missing package surfaces.
fix_comment: Still open and tracked by FTR-REVIEW-001-MODULARIZATION-P4A. A current LOC check still finds 6 src modules >500 lines and 11 >440 lines, including domain/core modules, so this is not closed; later package-split methodology is useful precedent but not closure evidence.

summary: Two validation finding models coexist with different error-policy semantics.
detail: The consolidation keeps this as a standalone should-fix item.
fix_comment: REVIEW-002 kept D-005 open, then FTR-FINDING-MODEL-CONSOLIDATION-P5 removed the duplicated finding-frame mechanics and was independently accepted.

summary: transformations/helpers.py mixes closure-factory style with unrelated helper responsibilities.
detail: Standalone should-fix item in the consolidation.
fix_comment: Still open. No dedicated FTR found. REVIEW-002 lists transformations/helpers.py paradigm mix as unchanged; later package/domain reviews still treat transformations/helpers.py as a structural anomaly and identify a future split candidate C003.

summary: yaml_overrides imports private helpers from meta_bootstrap.
detail: Also listed as a quick-win candidate in the consolidation.
fix_comment: Quick-wins review verifies _deep_merge/_get_meta/_set_meta were made public and yaml_overrides imports public names. D-013 interaction remains separate.

summary: validations/validate_columns.py adds constraints rather than validating columns.
detail: The consolidation treats this as module placement and naming debt.
fix_comment: Still open and now tracked by FTR-REVIEW-001-META-BOUNDARY-CLEANUP-P5. Current validate_columns.py still exposes add_validations as a metadata-producing constraint writer, so the naming/ownership finding is not closed.

summary: Legacy FK-helper compatibility branches and a broad exception handler remain in core.
detail: Cluster C2 notes this may be resolved together with FK ownership cleanup.
fix_comment: Partially addressed by FK-helper v1 retirement and policy-driven primitives, but the original compat-residue evidence is not fully closed: core/fk.py still carries legacy dict input handling and core/refs.py still has a broad except. Keep open for detailed review.

summary: Domain and rendering package surfaces are empty or inconsistent.
detail: Raw list says should-fix; prioritized overview places it in HYGIENE under C7.
fix_comment: Partially mitigated for newer split packages, but REVIEW-002 says the original empty/inconsistent package surfaces remain. Keep current/open.

summary: Domain code performs file I/O and directly depends on pyyaml without a guard.
detail: Standalone should-fix item in the consolidation.
fix_comment: Still open and now tracked by FTR-REVIEW-001-META-BOUNDARY-CLEANUP-P5. Current domain code still imports yaml and performs file I/O in structured_yaml.py, yaml_overrides.py, and related domain helpers; this is not closed by comment alone.

summary: Domain and core modules lack a consistent module-header convention.
detail: Raw list says nice-to-have; prioritized overview groups D-012 and IR-014 as HYGIENE cluster C8.
fix_comment: Tracked by the module-headers FTR, but no implementation/closure found. REVIEW-002 lists D-012/IR-014 as unchanged.

summary: meta_bootstrap and validate_columns still contain a dual-interface Frames shim.
detail: Raw list says nice-to-have; prioritized overview places it in HYGIENE.
fix_comment: Still open and now tracked by FTR-REVIEW-001-META-BOUNDARY-CLEANUP-P5. meta_bootstrap.py and validate_columns.py still carry the dual-interface convention, including frames.meta and frames["_meta"] handling.

summary: enrich_lookup formula mode treats missing_mode branches identically, making missing_mode a no-op.
detail: Clustered with C1 because the same enrich_lookup touch area is affected by the formula boundary move.
fix_comment: The formula-mode missing_mode no-op was fixed in the formulas/core-move slice; REVIEW-002 groups D-014 among resolved quick/follow-up items.

summary: Rendering and pipeline review found no separate release-blocking issue beyond D-001 and IR-002.
detail: Kept as a historical non-finding row because it appears in the consolidation Rohliste.
fix_comment: Historical non-finding row. No separate fix required beyond the D-001/IR-002 formula-boundary closure.

summary: rendering/formulas.py contains backend-neutral FormulaSpec value objects and factory logic in the wrong layer.
detail: Prioritized overview treats D-001 and IR-002 together as C1/CRITICAL.
fix_comment: FormulaSpec and factories moved to core/formulas.py; REVIEW-002 marks D-001 + IR-002 resolved and guarded.

summary: rendering/passes contains misleading stubs while real pass logic is bundled in core.py.
detail: Cluster C3 groups pass-package cleanup with pass-order deduplication.
fix_comment: rendering/passes was split into per-pass modules; dead/stub core/formula/post pass structure removed. REVIEW-002 marks IR-003 + IR-004 resolved and guarded.

summary: Two pass-order sources are maintained manually without a drift test.
detail: Cluster C3 groups pass-order deduplication with pass-package cleanup.
fix_comment: default_passes() became the single pass-order source with ordering tests; REVIEW-002 marks IR-003 + IR-004 resolved and guarded.

summary: The derived meta channel crosses domain and rendering without a meta_registry contract.
detail: Cluster C6 covers registering or explicitly documenting the derived meta channel.
fix_comment: The derived meta channel is registered and guard-tested. The FTR review explicitly says all three parts of IR-005 are resolved.

summary: Render path mutates caller metadata and frames in place.
detail: Standalone should-fix item in the prioritized overview.
fix_comment: REVIEW-002 carried IR-006 as open, then the dedicated FTR fixed render input mutation; review accepted commit 865eecc with no findings.

summary: Unused pipeline step factories and wrapper constants duplicate registry target strings.
detail: Also listed as a quick-win cleanup candidate.
fix_comment: Quick-wins review verifies unused flatten/unflatten step factories were removed and drift vector is gone.

summary: workbook_meta_blob is written as Python repr and read through JSON/literal fallback.
detail: Duplicate of A-004 from IR perspective; prioritized as C4/CRITICAL.
fix_comment: workbook_meta_blob write path switched from Python repr to json.dumps with ADR/test coverage; REVIEW-002 marks IR-008 + A-004 resolved.

summary: rendering/__init__.py is empty, forcing consumers to know deep paths.
detail: Raw list says nice-to-have; prioritized overview places it in HYGIENE under C7.
fix_comment: Tracked with C7 modularization, but no direct closure found. REVIEW-002 says newer package surfaces improved while original rendering/domain package surfaces remained incomplete.

summary: pipeline/registry.py mixes registry mapping, build process, and run_pipeline re-export.
detail: Prioritized overview treats this as hygiene overlapping C5.
fix_comment: pipeline/registry.py was split into registry/build/execution/runner responsibilities; REVIEW-002 marks A-001/A-010/IR-010/IR-012 resolved.

summary: FK step factory names do not match visible registry keys.
detail: Prioritized overview treats this as hygiene adjacent to FK consolidation.
fix_comment: Public YAML step names were normalized by FTR-PIPELINE-STEP-NAMING-P4, but the original internal factory-name mismatch remains visible: make_apply_fks_step, make_drop_helpers_step, and make_check_fk_helpers_step still differ from registry keys. Keep open under FTR-DOMAIN-CONSOLIDATION.

summary: A second AppConfig/PipelineConfig model exists beside the step-based build configuration.
detail: Prioritized overview treats this as hygiene overlapping C5.
fix_comment: Slice02 removed StepRef/PipelineConfig parallel dialect and converged AppConfig on the canonical step-based spec list; REVIEW-002 marks this resolved.

summary: MetaPass reserves exported_at but no package path fills it.
detail: Listed as NICE-TO-HAVE in the prioritized overview; also appears as a possible quick decision.
fix_comment: Still open and now tracked by FTR-REVIEW-001-META-BOUNDARY-CLEANUP-P5. MetaPass still reserves exported_at with an empty default and no package path fills it; later payload work treats it as workbook identity but does not close the ownership decision.

summary: Rendering and pipeline modules lack a consistent module-header convention.
detail: Raw list says nice-to-have; prioritized overview groups D-012 and IR-014 as HYGIENE cluster C8.
fix_comment: Tracked by the module-headers FTR, but no implementation/closure found. REVIEW-002 lists D-012/IR-014 as unchanged.

summary: Backend dispatch is duplicated across router, io_backends factory, and application orchestrator.
detail: Cluster C5 groups backend dispatch, orchestrator, registry, and config-model cleanup.
fix_comment: Backend dispatch is centralized through router/loader/saver ownership and pipeline responsibilities were split. REVIEW-002 marks A-001/A-010/IR-010/IR-012 resolved.

summary: sheets-pack advertises csv_dir input but orchestrate lacks a CSV load path.
detail: Can be fixed minimally or as part of C5 backend dispatch consolidation.
fix_comment: The misleading sheets-pack/unpack surface was removed/approved as pre-beta cleanup and orchestrate now routes CSV via the canonical loader path. REVIEW-002 marks A-002 resolved.

summary: Simple XML/YAML writers do not skip _meta and can crash on meta-producing pipelines.
detail: Raw list says should-fix; prioritized overview promotes this to CRITICAL because it is a concrete writer crash.
fix_comment: Quick-wins review/follow-up verifies XML/YAML simple writers skip _meta and added non-crash tests; REVIEW-002 marks A-003 resolved.

summary: workbook_meta_blob is persisted as Python repr and read with ast.literal_eval fallback.
detail: Duplicate of IR-008 from adapter perspective; prioritized as C4/CRITICAL.
fix_comment: workbook_meta_blob persistence moved to JSON with legacy read fallback/ADR/test coverage; REVIEW-002 marks IR-008 + A-004 resolved.

summary: CLI error UX wrapper and --debug path are bypassed by installed console_scripts.
detail: Standalone should-fix item in the prioritized overview.
fix_comment: Later CLI UX FTR rewired the installed sheets-run console script through cli_entry -> run_cli -> main, preserving debug/verbose tracebacks and adding entry-point regression tests. This closes the A-005 console_scripts wrapper issue for sheets-run.

summary: io_backends/_interfaces.py is a dead legacy re-export.
detail: Raw list says nice-to-have; prioritized overview places it in HYGIENE and quick wins.
fix_comment: Quick-wins review verifies io_backends/_interfaces.py was deleted and no import path remains.

summary: coerce_backend_options(None) returns a dict cast as BackendOptions instead of BackendOptions().
detail: Raw list says nice-to-have; prioritized overview places it in HYGIENE and quick wins.
fix_comment: Quick-wins review verifies coerce_backend_options(None) now returns BackendOptions() and the required JSON writer follow-up was included.

summary: JSON read/write is not symmetric for MultiIndex frames.
detail: Listed under NICE-TO-HAVE in the prioritized overview.
fix_comment: Still open and now tracked by FTR-REVIEW-001-MULTIHEADER-READBACK-P5 as follow-up to FTR-MULTIHEADER-P2. Current JSON write supports nested MultiIndex output, but read_multi still ignores header_levels and does not rebuild symmetric MultiIndex readback.

summary: CSVBackend.read_multi ignores header_levels and loses real multi-header structure.
detail: Listed under NICE-TO-HAVE in the prioritized overview.
fix_comment: Still open and linked to the FTR-MULTIHEADER-P2 lineage. Current CSV read_multi still reads header=0 and constructs a padded fake MultiIndex instead of honoring header_levels for real multi-header CSV directory input.

summary: pipeline.runner.run_pipeline and pipeline.registry.run_pipeline share a name with different signatures.
detail: Raw list says nice-to-have; prioritized overview groups it with A-001 under SHOULD-FIX/C5.
fix_comment: pipeline.runner.run_pipeline(AppConfig) was renamed to run_app, and frames->frames execution lives in pipeline/execution.py; REVIEW-002 marks A-010 resolved.

summary: REVIEW-002 confirms IR-006 render-input mutation remains live and is the most important carry-over.
detail: Confirmed live at frame_selection.py and layout_composer.py; REVIEW-002 says it is a latent correctness hotspot on any second-output path.

summary: Module-size debt regressed in absolute terms after REVIEW-001.
detail: REVIEW-002 reports roughly 13 modules above about 430 lines versus REVIEW-001's nine above 440, with peak 801 lines.

summary: Architecture guard inventory no longer enumerates the live guard suite.
detail: Inventory dated 2026-04-24 omits newer domain_transformations and carrier_boundary guard families.

summary: odfpy moved from optional adapter extra to core runtime dependency and should be reflected in dependency guidance.
detail: REVIEW-002 treats this as deliberate and accepted, but notes tension with earlier lazy optional adapter guidance.

summary: ARCH-LAYERS still describes stale modules and pass order after the architecture moved on.
detail: ARCH-LAYERS still names pipeline/pipeline.py, core/fk.py as FK home, transformations/helpers.py for reorder, and a 5-pass order.

summary: Recurring tiny-helper duplication exists across several modules, but is a watch item rather than a defect.
detail: REVIEW-002 classifies this as minor fragmentation-adjacent under-consolidation, not an actionable defect.

summary: REVIEW-002 confirms core FK compatibility residue remains open and should be cleaned conservatively.
detail: core/fk.py legacy dict input and flat id->label paths plus core/refs.py broad exception remained visible in REVIEW-002.

summary: Finding-model duality is actually quadruplication across validation and transformation modules.
detail: Pass 2 identifies four independent finding dataclass / FINDING_COLUMNS / serializer shapes and makes consolidation prerequisite to packaging reference_validations.

summary: reference_validations and join_views duplicate a small where/condition predicate vocabulary.
detail: REVIEW-002 says two consumers do not justify a DSL package; revisit only at a third consumer.

summary: Domain transformation package-boundary guards cover only existing split families, not the remaining large modules.
detail: Any future split of the 11 scoped modules must ship re-export and boundary guards in the same slice.

summary: xref_crosstable.py is the highest-value package-split candidate, constrained by dense-axes and roundtrip invariants.
detail: Ranked #1, PROCEED WITH CONSTRAINTS: split only via discriminator-style methodology, preserving re-exports, dense witnesses, and chained roundtrip.

summary: workbook_views.py is a clean package-split candidate but should be timed with workbook-view contract ownership.
detail: Ranked #2, PROCEED WITH CONSTRAINTS: clean configure/readback seam, but split should align with active workbook-view mapping FTR ownership.

summary: helper_policies needs placement and contract-ownership analysis before any split.
detail: Ranked #4 as ANALYZE FIRST; future candidate is subsystem analysis across helper_policies, enrich_lookup, fk_helpers, and derived_column_policy.

summary: XLSX parser promotes authored column-width carrier observations into persisted canonical meta while ODS does not.
detail: Carrier column widths are quarantined as hints but also merged into sheets.<name>.column_widths and re-stored in workbook_meta_blob.

summary: Workbook option vocabulary is triplicated and already divergent across write and read paths.
detail: Composer owns a 5-key write vocabulary while XLSX/ODS parser interpretation modules use a 7-key read vocabulary including helper_columns and protection.

summary: The MultiIndex header separator " / " is an unowned magic-string contract duplicated across four modules.
detail: Composer joins with the literal separator, parsers join non-empty parts, workbook_projection splits on it, and no shared owner or guard exists.

summary: ODS renderer silently drops SetColumnWidth instead of documenting the accepted capability gap in code and tests.
detail: Protection gaps have explicit no-op branches and tests; SetColumnWidth falls through dispatch silently.

summary: Pass 3 confirms the IR-006 mutation is reachable through the shared multi-backend write facade.
detail: The cross-adapter parity harness writes XLSX and ODS from the same frames/meta objects; the first write can mutate state consumed by the second.

summary: ODS hidden-sheet detection hard-codes _meta; REVIEW-002 accepts this as deliberate pragmatism.
detail: The literal _meta fallback is accepted because _meta is a reserved frame key and the ODS capability profile mandates the carrier name.

summary: ODS semantic parity is strong at integration tier but not pinned by a fast architecture-tier guard.
detail: Pass 3 suggests either promoting one minimal ODS roundtrip invariant to architecture tier or recording a deliberate GDR accepting integration-tier parity with review cadence.
